example_discussion_questions: Are you shaped more by your culture or by your individual personality?
Can you think of something from YOUR culture that visitors find surprising or confusing?
Is it possible to fully understand a culture you did not grow up in?
When two people from different cultures argue, is it usually about something above or below the waterline?
Is your culture changing? What is staying the same and what is different from your parents' generation?
1 customer goes to the order speaker every 1 minute.
Are these techniques honest or manipulative?
Is there a difference between persuasion and manipulation?
Where else do these techniques appear -- politics, social media, job interviews?
If someone buys something they don't need because of urgency tactics, whose fault is that?
Should there be laws limiting what presenters can say on live TV?
example_vocab_items: [IMAGE: Live TV studio countdown clock showing 1:30]
💬  Groups of 3 - you have 12 minutes to prepare
Your challenge: sell it on live TV in exactly 90 seconds.
The class will vote after each pitch: Would you buy it?
example_gap_fill_sentences: 1. In Japan, there is an ___ that you should be quiet on public transport.
2. Many Asian cultures are more ___ -- family decisions are made together.
3. It is a ___ in many cultures to ask someone their salary directly.
4. Bowing when you meet someone is part of Japanese social ___.
example_teacher_instructions: Read out the instructions to the class. Split the class into groups an have them rank the food trends and then share their ideas and agree on a ranking as a class. Give some feedback.

Read out the instructions of the task to the class. Students will work in groups of 3 and invent their own viral TikTok food. They will need to come up with a name, description, visual concept and cre
Split the class into groups and then show them the peer review and class vote slides. Make sure they understand everything and then let them go off and complete the activity. Once they have finished,

Go to the next slide and read out the instructions. Split the class into 4 teams. Read out each sentence, teams then vote for above or below the waterline. Reveal the answer after both teams have vote

Tell the class that they will be working in their groups and will need to discuss 2 questions and then share the most interesting point that came up with the rest of the class. Give the groups a few m

Tell the class that they will now draw their own iceberg. Read out the instructions to the class and ask some ICQs. Go to the next slide and show the example UK iceberg and then let students make thei

Tell each student what role they are (teacher, teenager, manager, doctor, child etc.) and then split the class into groups of 4. Students will need to recommend some useful apps to the rest of the stu
Before starting, Ss can have a couple of minutes to research some apps.
